compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, ambiguity and conflict interactions halt brainstorm for resolution
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Ambiguity and conflict classifications stop the brainstorm pipeline and present structured resolution options to the practitioner, with re-scan bounded by config, non-interactive deferral via tag, and entry gates in downstream skills.
**Why**: Ensures that overlapping resource claims and mutually exclusive strategies are resolved at planning time, not discovered at runtime.
**Scope**:
- IN: Halt mechanism, resolution paths (namespace owner vs shared usage for ambiguity; authoritative approach for conflict), re-scan after resolution bounded by brainstorm.max_interaction_cycles (default: 2), INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED signal following replan-observability contract, interaction:deferred tag with full lifecycle (writer + readers + removal), preplanning and implementation-plan entry gates, sprint handling of interaction:deferred halt
- OUT: Classification logic (S1), AC injection (S2)

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, ambiguity interactions present two resolution options: (a) designate one epic as namespace owner, or (b) accept shared usage with an explicit integration contract written as AC
  <- Satisfies: "On ambiguity: the practitioner selects from..."
- When this story is complete, conflict interactions require choosing one approach as authoritative, with the other epic flagged for approach revision
  <- Satisfies: "On conflict: the practitioner chooses one approach as authoritative"
- When this story is complete, re-scans after resolution are bounded by brainstorm.max_interaction_cycles config key (default: 2 when absent, following brainstorm.max_feasibility_cycles pattern)
  <- Satisfies: "re-runs bounded by brainstorm.max_interaction_cycles"
- When this story is complete, non-interactive sessions record INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED following the replan-observability contract format and tag the epic interaction:deferred
  <- Satisfies: "non-interactive mode records INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED"
- When this story is complete, the interaction:deferred tag has a documented lifecycle contract: writer (non-interactive brainstorm), readers (preplanning and implementation-plan entry gates), removal (interactive brainstorm re-run resolving the deferred finding removes the tag)
  <- Satisfies: "preplanning and implementation-plan entry gates check for this tag and block decomposition"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Maintainability] Gate changes span brainstorm SKILL.md, preplanning SKILL.md, and implementation-plan SKILL.md — coordinate changes
- [Maintainability] Sprint SKILL.md also invokes preplanning/implementation-plan — sprint must handle the case where those skills halt at an interaction:deferred gate. Follow the existing design:awaiting_import filtering pattern in sprint
- [Reliability] When both FEASIBILITY_GAP and interaction halt fire on the same epic, interaction halt should run first (before scrutiny pipeline re-entry) since approach changes from interaction resolution may resolve feasibility gaps. Document this ordering
- [Maintainability] INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED signal must use the format from replan-observability contract to be discoverable by sprint/debug-everything resume logic

## Escalation Policy
Escalation policy: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue that could send implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. All assumptions made without escalating are documented.
